- sex / gender: Male
- age: 67
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Tappers, Other
- education_level: Below Primary
- zone: Rural
- state: Karnataka
- district: Hassan
- first_language: Kannada
- clinical_persona_summary: A 67-year-old male from rural Karnataka, currently married, with below-primary education and a first language of Kannada. He works as a palm tapper and is concerned about health, finances, and the future. His lifestyle includes daily consumption of coconut water and occasional alcohol, with limited mobility due to age.

Document format guide for Automated SMS Notifications (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
CRITICAL: keep the WHOLE SMS under ~400 characters / 2–4 sentences. Not a form dump.
Example:
[[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]: appt [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]] at [[HOSPITAL_NAME|PHC Kanpur]] on 21-May 10:30 with [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sharma]]. Confirm on [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]].

- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- APPOINTMENT_ID — Appointment ID e.g. [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

professional_persona: Samina Aadin, an aspiring commerce diploma student from the Bangalore outskirts, channels their household budgeting acumen and basic computer literacy into collaborative class projects, while their relaxed, flexible attitude lets them juggle studies with family chores.
linguistic_persona: Samina Aadin, a native Kannada speaker fully literate in the language and proficient in basic English reading and writing, uses their smartphone and simple Microsoft Office skills to manage school assignments and stay connected on social media.
sports_persona: Samina Aadin, a laid‑back cricket fan who roots for Royal Challengers Bangalore and joins neighborhood badminton sessions at the local community hall, they enjoy informal weekend games more than regimented training.
arts_persona: Samina Aadin, an avid listener of Kannada folk singer Vijay Prakash and Bollywood vocalists Arijit Singh and Shreya Ghoshal, they stream the latest songs while studying and attend local Yakshagana performances for cultural enrichment.
travel_persona: Samina Aadin, an inquisitive weekend traveler who plans budget‑friendly trips to Nandi Hills for sunrise picnics, explores Mysore's palace during school holidays, and dreams of a future road‑trip to Goa's beaches with friends, they balance adventure with family commitments.
culinary_persona: Samina Aadin, a modest home cook who loves preparing Bisi Bele Bath with extra veggies, crisp Ragi Mudde, tangy coconut chutney, and occasionally experimenting with masala dosa fillings, they always add fresh coriander for aromatic zest.
persona: Samina Aadin, a 19‑year‑old balancing studies, family duties, and weekend music, they are a relaxed planner who saves every rupee for future education yet occasionally misplaces deadlines.
cultural_background: Samina belongs to the Muslim community in the rural outskirts of Bangalore, Karnataka. Her cultural upbringing blends the Kannada customs of the region—such as local festivals, cuisine, and community gatherings—with Islamic traditions observed by her family. She grows up in a joint family setting where respect for elders, hospitality, and modesty are central, and she experiences a mix of Kannada folklore and the religious practices of Eid and Ramadan.
linguistic_background: Samina's mother tongue is Kannada, in which she is fully literate and can converse fluently. Her formal schooling has given her basic proficiency in English—enough to read textbooks, write simple essays, and communicate in daily interactions—but she does not use English regularly outside school. She has no formal knowledge of any other language.
skills_and_expertise: Samina has developed practical household management skills, including basic cooking, laundry, and budgeting for a small family. She possesses native-level proficiency in Kannada and basic computer literacy, allowing her to use a smartphone, social media, and simple Microsoft Office applications. Her interpersonal strengths include active listening, empathy, and conflict resolution within her family and peer group.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Household management", "Basic cooking", "Laundry", "Budgeting", "Kannada language proficiency", "Basic computer literacy", "Active listening", "Empathy", "Conflict resolution"]
hobbies_and_interests: Samina enjoys listening to Kannada folk music and contemporary Bollywood songs, exploring traditional recipes during weekends, and spending time in her family's garden where she tends to herbs and seasonal vegetables. She also likes reading simple storybooks in Kannada, chatting with friends on social media, and participating in community events during festivals.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Listening to Kannada folk music", "Contemporary Bollywood songs", "Cooking traditional recipes", "Garden gardening (herbs and vegetables)", "Reading Kannada storybooks", "Social media chatting with friends", "Participating in community festivals"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Samina aims to continue her education beyond matriculation, aspiring to enroll in a diploma or degree program in commerce or computer applications that can open up opportunities for a stable job or a small family-run business. She envisions using her practical skills to support her family financially while maintaining a flexible schedule that allows her to care for household responsibilities and participate in community activities.
persona_clinical_summary: A 19‑year‑old female from rural Karnataka, never married, with a matriculation education who is currently a homemaker. She speaks Kannada as her first language and has basic English proficiency, living in the Bangalore Rural district. Her lifestyle includes saving money for future education and managing household budgets.

ಪ್ರಿಸ್ಕ್ರಿಪ್ಷನ್ — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Karnataka Institute of Medical Sciences]]
ರೋಗಿ [[PATIENT_NAME|Shanthi]], [[AGE|19]] / [[GENDER|Female]], MRN [[MRN|RX-2024-0098]]
ಡಾ. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya N. Rao]]
ಫೋನ್: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]

ರೋಗಿ ಐಡಿ: [[PATIENT_ID|PID-KR-2024-0098]]
ABHA ಐಡಿ: [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Rx:
Tab. Paracetamol 500mg - ಜ್ವರಕ್ಕಾಗಿ ಊಟದ ನಂತರ ದಿನಕ್ಕೆ ಎರಡು ಬಾರಿ ಎರಡು ಮಾತ್ರೆಗಳು.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

user_prompt:
Write one complete Discharge Summary for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Malayalam (code=ml, script=Malayalam). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 43
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Literate without education level
- zone: Urban
- state: Kerala
- district: Pathanamthitta
- first_language: Malayalam
- clinical_persona_summary: A 43-year-old female from Pathanamthitta, Kerala, who is never married, is retired and literate without a formal education. She lives in an urban zone and speaks Malayalam, reflecting a Malayali Christian cultural background. Her lifestyle includes meticulous budgeting, a thriving kitchen garden, and community-focused activities.

Document format guide for Discharge Summary (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] DOB [[DOB|1994-06-21]] [[AGE|30]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]] | Course / advice: <concise paragraphs matching domain + persona sex/age>

MANDATORY inline entities (14 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- ABHA_ADDRESS — ABHA Address e.g. [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
- CASTE — Caste e.g. [[CASTE|OBC]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

professional_persona: Mohammed Haneefa, a 71‑year‑old retired community elder and volunteer bookkeeper, blends meticulous household finance management, dependable dispute mediation and expert home cooking of traditional Kerala dishes, and they embody a routine‑driven, compassionate, and socially engaged personality.
linguistic_persona: Mohammed Haneefa, fluent in Malayalam, can read and write it effortlessly, knows basic Arabic for Quranic recitation, and uses functional English for everyday documents, and they often mix Malayalam and English while conversing.
sports_persona: Mohammed Haneefa enjoys daily walks in the local park, follows the Kerala Blasters in the Indian Super League, and participates in a senior citizens' gentle yoga group, preferring low‑impact activities that keep them fit and socially engaged.
arts_persona: Mohammed Haneefa loves listening to classic coastal folk songs sung by artists such as K.J. Yesudas and K.S. Radhakrishnan, watches Malayalam cinema featuring actors like Mammootty and Mohanlal, and regularly attends local cultural gatherings where they enjoy traditional poetry recitations and stage dramas.
travel_persona: Mohammed Haneefa enjoys weekend trips to the backwaters of Alappuzha, occasional pilgrimages to Sabarimala, and dreams of a serene houseboat cruise through Kumarakom, while balancing travel costs with a modest pension.
culinary_persona: Mohammed Haneefa excels at preparing traditional Kerala Muslim dishes such as biryani, shavri, fish curry on a banana leaf, and savoury beef fry, relishes coconut‑laden flavors, and prefers home‑cooked meals though they occasionally indulge in sweet Payasam after festivals.
persona: Mohammed Haneefa, a 71‑year‑old retired community pillar, blends routine‑driven compassion with occasional forgetfulness, keeping neighbours happy through cooking, prayer, and gentle counsel.
cultural_background: Born and raised in the coastal state of Kerala, Mohammed Haneefa belongs to the Mappila Muslim community of Thiruvananthapuram. The Mappila culture blends Malayalam language, Arab-influenced cuisine, and centuries-old maritime trade traditions, and it places strong emphasis on family routines, modesty and communal harmony. Living in an urban neighbourhood, his daily life is organised around regular prayers, shared family meals of rice, fish and coconut-based dishes, and the yearly observances of Ramadan and Eid, which provide the familiar rhythm he values.
linguistic_background: His mother-tongue is Malayalam, which he can read and write comfortably thanks to his primary-school education. He also knows a modest amount of Arabic for reciting the Quran, though he does not consider himself fluent. English functions as his secondary language; he understands spoken English well and can read simple documents such as ration cards, medicine labels and short letters, often mixing Malayalam and English in everyday conversation. He has no knowledge of any third language.
skills_and_expertise: Over his lifetime he has developed practical skills deeply rooted in his household and community roles. He is an excellent home-cook, especially of traditional Kerala Muslim dishes such as biryani, shavri, and banana-leaf served fish curry. He manages household finances on a modest budget, keeping track of monthly expenses and ration allocations. He possesses basic first-aid knowledge, enabling him to attend to minor injuries of family members. His proficiency in reading Malayalam script allows him to help grandchildren with school work and to transcribe community notices. He also serves as a trusted mediator in neighbourhood disputes, leveraging his calm demeanor and respected standing. Additionally, he can recite short passages of the Quran and lead prayers when needed.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['Traditional Kerala Muslim cooking', 'Household budgeting', 'Basic first aid', 'Literacy support for grandchildren', 'Community dispute mediation', 'Quran recitation', 'Mosque volunteering']
hobbies_and_interests: He enjoys listening to classic Mappila folk songs (Mappila Paattu), attending local cultural gatherings, taking daily walks in the nearby park, tending a small balcony garden of banana plants and aromatic herbs, playing traditional card games such as Satte with friends, and reading Malayalam newspapers or short story magazines. During Ramadan and Eid he gladly participates in community feasts, and occasionally watches popular Malayalam movies on television.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Listening to classic Mappila folk songs', 'Attending local cultural gatherings', 'Daily walks in the park', 'Balcony gardening of banana plants and herbs', 'Playing traditional card games like Satte', 'Reading Malayalam newspapers or short story magazines', 'Participating in community feasts during Ramadan and Eid', 'Occasionally watching Malayalam movies on television']
career_goals_and_ambitions: He may be officially retired, but he still sees himself as a pillar of his family and local community. His main ambition is to pass on his culinary knowledge and cultural stories to his grandchildren, ensuring the Mappila heritage lives on. He also wishes to volunteer regularly at his mosque, guiding young members in basic Quranic recitation and helping with the mosque’s modest charitable fund bookkeeping. Maintaining his health through daily walks, simple yoga, and learning to use a smartphone for video calls with distant relatives is another personal goal. He hopes to stay socially active, fostering harmony among neighbours and contributing to local charitable initiatives.

His first language is Malayalam, with limited Arabic and English proficiency. He follows a routine-driven lifestyle, engaging in daily walks and gentle yoga, while managing household finances and mediating community disputes.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

user_prompt:
Write one complete Discharge Summary for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Malayalam (code=ml, script=Malayalam). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 71
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Urban
- state: Kerala
- district: Thiruvananthapuram
- first_language: Malayalam
- clinical_persona_summary: A 71-year-old male, currently married, retired, with primary education, living in an urban area of Kerala. His first language is Malayalam, with limited Arabic and English proficiency. He follows a routine-driven lifestyle, engaging in daily walks and gentle yoga, while managing household finances and mediating community disputes.

Document format guide for Discharge Summary (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] DOB [[DOB|1994-06-21]] [[AGE|30]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]] | Course / advice: <concise paragraphs matching domain + persona sex/age>

MANDATORY inline entities (14 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- ABHA_ADDRESS — ABHA Address e.g. [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
- CASTE — Caste e.g. [[CASTE|OBC]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
ഡിസ്ചാർജ് സമ്മറി — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|മരിയ ഹോസ്പിറ്റൽ]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|രമേശ് കുമാർ]] ജനനത്തീയതി [[DOB|1953-08-15]] [[AGE|71]] [[GENDER|Male]]
അഡ്മിഷൻ [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-1028]] വാർഡ് [[WARD_NUMBER|A1]] ബെഡ് [[BED_NUMBER|05]]
ഡോ. [[DOCTOR_NAME|ഡോ. അനിത ശർമ്മ]] | ചികിത്സാ രീതി / നിർദ്ദേശങ്ങൾ: രോഗിയെ പൾമണറി ട്യൂബർകുലോസിസ് (Pulmonary tuberculosis) രോഗനിർണ്ണയത്തോടെ പ്രവേശിപ്പിച്ചു. ഇതിനോടൊപ്പം ടൈപ്പ് 2 ഡയബറ്റിസ് മെലിറ്റസും (type 2 diabetes mellitus) കണ്ടെത്തി. അദ്ദേഹം ലെവോഫ്ലോക്സാസിൻ (levofloxacin), ബെഡാക്വിലൈൻ (bedaquiline), ലൈനെസോളിഡ് (linezolid), സൈക്ലോസെറിൻ (cycloserine) എന്നീ മരുന്നുകൾ ഉപയോഗിച്ച് 9 മാസത്തെ തീവ്രമായ ചികിത്സാ ഘട്ടം പൂർത്തിയാക്കി. തുടർന്ന് ചികിത്സാ ഘട്ടവും പൂർത്തിയാക്കി. രക്തത്തിലെ പഞ്ചസാരയുടെ അളവ് നിരീക്ഷിക്കുകയും മെറ്റ്‌ഫോമിൻ (metformin), ഇൻസുലിൻ എന്നിവ ഉപയോഗിച്ച് നിയന്ത്രിക്കുകയും ചെയ്തു. റിവൈസ്ഡ് നാഷണൽ ട്യൂബർകുലോസിസ് കൺട്രോൾ പ്രോഗ്രാം (RNTCP) മാർഗ്ഗനിർദ്ദേശങ്ങൾ പ്രകാരം ആന്റി-ട്യൂബർകുലർ തെറാപ്പി (anti-tubercular therapy) പൂർണ്ണമായും തുടരാൻ നിർദ്ദേശിച്ചിട്ടുണ്ട്. പോഷകസമൃദ്ധമായ ഭക്ഷണം കഴിക്കുക, പുകവലിയും മദ്യപാനവും ഒഴിവാക്കുക, നടത്തം പോലുള്ള ലഘുവായ ശാരീരിക വ്യായാമങ്ങളിൽ ഏർപ്പെടുക എന്നിവ നിർദ്ദേശിക്കുന്നു. ഒരു മാസത്തിന് ശേഷം ചെസ്റ്റ് മെഡിസിൻ വിഭാഗത്തിൽ ഫോളോ-അപ്പ് നിശ്ചയിച്ചിട്ടുണ്ട്. രോഗിയുടെ ഭാര്യ [[RELATIVE_NAME|സീത]], ചികിത്സാ കൃത്യനിഷ്ഠയുടെ പ്രാധാന്യത്തെക്കുറിച്ച് കൗൺസിലിംഗ് നൽകി. രോഗിയുടെ ABHA ID [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]] ഉം ABHA വിലാസം [[ABHA_ADDRESS|gopalakrishnan.k@abdm]] ഉം ആണ്. ഫോൺ നമ്പർ [[PHONE_NUMBER|9447012345]]. രോഗി കേരളത്തിലെ [[DISTRICT|വയനാട്]] ജില്ലയിലുള്ള [[CASTE|നായർ]] സമുദായത്തിൽപ്പെട്ടയാളാണ്.
generated_text_en:
Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Government Medical College Hospital, Thiruvananthapuram]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|K. Gopalakrishnan]] DOB [[DOB|1953-08-15]] [[AGE|71]] [[GENDER|Male]]
Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-1028]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|A1]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|05]]
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sreekantan Nair]] | Course / advice: The patient was admitted with a diagnosis of pulmonary tuberculosis with multidrug-resistant (MDR-TB) and concurrent type 2 diabetes mellitus. He has completed a 9-month intensive phase of treatment with levofloxacin, bedaquiline, linezolid, and cycloserine, followed by a continuation phase. His blood sugar levels have been monitored and managed with metformin and insulin as required. The patient is advised to continue the full course of anti-tubercular therapy as per the Revised National Tuberculosis Control Programme (RNTCP) guidelines. He should maintain a nutritious diet, avoid smoking and alcohol, and engage in light physical activity like walking. Follow-up is scheduled with the chest medicine department in one month. The patient's wife, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Lakshmi Gopalakrishnan]], has been counseled on the importance of treatment adherence. The patient's ABHA ID is [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]] and his ABHA address is [[ABHA_ADDRESS|gopalakrishnan.k@abdm]]. His phone number is [[PHONE_NUMBER|9447012345]]. The patient belongs to the [[CASTE|Ezhava]] community and resides in the [[DISTRICT|Thiruvananthapuram]] district of Kerala.

professional_persona: A seasoned motorcycle driver and part‑time mechanic, Meshram Chaudhary blends meticulous route planning, reliable bike maintenance, and a competitive edge in local delivery races as they diligently pursue a commercial driving license to grow a small fleet.
linguistic_persona: Meshram Chaudhary speaks Marathi fluently, converses comfortably in Hindi, and handles everyday English phrases, relying on practical communication for road navigation and simple paperwork.
sports_persona: Meshram Chaudhary enjoys weekend cricket matches with village friends, rooting for the Mumbai Indians in IPL while maintaining a modest fitness routine that includes short jogs after rides.
arts_persona: Meshram Chaudhary often listens to Marathi folk tunes by artists like Shahir Sable and Vithabai Bhau Mang, while unwinding with comic books such as Chacha Chaudhary and Nagraj, finding cultural resonance in both.
travel_persona: Meshram Chaudhary prefers short road trips to nearby hill stations like Lonavala and Mahabaleshwar, planning routes meticulously to balance fuel costs, while occasionally traveling to Pune for bike parts, favoring practical over luxury travel.
culinary_persona: Meshram Chaudhary enjoys the fresh vegetable dishes like bhindi and aloo grown in his modest garden, and relishes spicy street snacks such as vada pav, misal pav, and pithla bhakri, where the bold flavors of chilies, coriander and kokum delight his palate.
persona: Meshram Chaudhary is a pragmatic, competitive rider who balances meticulous planning with a love for Marathi folk tunes, occasional comic book escapes, and a disciplined, budget‑savvy lifestyle.
cultural_background: Meshram Chaudhary belongs to a Marathi-speaking rural community in Wardha, Maharashtra. His family follows traditional Maharashtrian customs, with deep ties to agriculture, village festivals, and Hindu rituals characteristic of central Maharashtra.
linguistic_background: Meshram's mother tongue is Marathi, which he reads and writes at a functional level thanks to his primary schooling. He is fluent in spoken Hindi and can manage basic reading and writing in Hindi. His English competency is limited to everyday conversational phrases and reading simple signage, reflecting his practical orientation and need for navigation on the road.
skills_and_expertise: Meshram is skilled in operating and maintaining motorcycles, including basic mechanical repairs, routine servicing, and troubleshooting. He possesses strong route planning abilities, time management, and safe driving practices tailored to rural road conditions. His experience also includes courteous customer interaction, handling payments, and managing a modest schedule of deliveries or passenger rides.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['Motorcycle operation', 'Basic mechanical repair', 'Routine vehicle maintenance', 'Route planning', 'Time management', 'Customer service', 'Cash handling', 'Safe driving in rural conditions']
hobbies_and_interests: In his free time, Meshram enjoys listening to Marathi folk music, playing cricket with friends from the village, reading comic books, tending to a small vegetable garden, and tinkering with motorcycle parts as a hobby.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Listening to Marathi folk music', 'Playing cricket', 'Reading comic books', 'Gardening', 'Motorcycle tinkering']
career_goals_and_ambitions: He aims to formalize his driving profession by obtaining a commercial driving license and expanding his services into a small fleet of motorcycles for local deliveries. Over the next five years, he hopes to increase his earnings, purchase a motorbike garage, and provide stable financial support for his family, while also mentoring younger riders in the village.
persona_clinical_summary: Mr. Meshram Chaudhary, a 36-year-old male, is a motorcycle driver from Wardha, Maharashtra, with primary education and a rural occupation. He is married, speaks Marathi as his first language, and is currently in a stable relationship. His lifestyle is budget-conscious, with a disciplined routine that includes listening to Marathi folk music and occasional comic books.

सध्याच्या आजाराचा इतिहास: रुग्णाने गेल्या तीन आठवड्यांपासून खोकल्याची तक्रार केली आहे, जो सुरुवातीला कोरडा होता आणि आता त्यात रक्ताच्या रेषा दिसत आहेत. त्याला संध्याकाळी हलका ताप येतो आणि गेल्या महिन्यात त्याचे वजन सुमारे ४ किलोने कमी झाले आहे. तो विश्रांतीच्या वेळी छातीत दुखणे किंवा धाप लागणे नाकारतो.
तपासणी: सामान्य स्थिती समाधानकारक आहे. जीवनचिन्हे: बीपी १२०/८० एमएमएचजी, नाडी ८८/मिनिट, तापमान ९९.२°फॅ, खोलीतील हवेवर SpO2 ९६%. छातीची तपासणी केल्यावर उजव्या वरच्या भागात श्वासाचा आवाज कमी आणि अधूनमधून रॉन्ची (rhonchi) असल्याचे दिसून येते. बोटांची टोके जाड (clubbing) झालेली नाहीत.
तपासण्या: एएफबीसाठी थुंकी पाठवली. छातीचा एक्स-रे उजव्या वरच्या भागात पोकळी (cavitary lesion) असल्याचे दर्शवतो.
निदान: फुफ्फुसाचा क्षयरोग, उजव्या वरच्या भागात पोकळी.
उपचार योजना: आरएनटीसीपी मार्गदर्शक तत्त्वांनुसार कॅटेगरी I डीओटीएस उपचार पद्धती सुरू केली. रुग्णाला औषधांचे नियमित सेवन आणि दुष्परिणामांबद्दल समुपदेशन केले.
पुढील भेट: २ आठवड्यांनी.

रुग्णालय कर्मचारी आयडी: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-45678]]

District: [[DISTRICT|Wardha]] | Chief complaint: Persistent cough with hemoptysis for 3 weeks.
History of Present Illness: Patient reports a productive cough for the past three weeks, initially dry and now with streaks of blood. He also complains of low-grade fever in the evenings and weight loss of approximately 4 kg over the last month. He denies any chest pain or shortness of breath at rest.
Examination: General condition is fair. Vitals: BP 120/80 mmHg, Pulse 88/min, Temp 99.2°F, SpO2 96% on room air. Chest examination reveals decreased breath sounds in the right upper zone with occasional rhonchi. No clubbing noted.
Investigations: Sputum for AFB sent. Chest X-ray shows a cavitary lesion in the right upper lobe.
Diagnosis: Pulmonary Tuberculosis, Right Upper Lobe Cavitary Lesion.
Treatment Plan: Started on Category I DOTS regimen as per RNTCP guidelines. Patient counseled on medication adherence and side effects.
Follow-up: Next appointment in 2 weeks.

Hospital Staff ID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-45678]]

professional_persona: A retired community organizer and volunteer, Dhok Jayashree leverages their meticulous budgeting, love for crafts, and organized nature; they run a senior women's art club and coordinate local cultural events.
linguistic_persona: Dhok Jayashree is fluent in Marathi, can read basic Hindi, and manages functional English for newspaper reading; they prefer Marathi for daily conversation.
sports_persona: As an enthusiastic walker and yoga practitioner, Dhok Jayashree follows Mumbai Indians cricket, joins a local senior walking club for morning strolls, and they treat gardening as a gentle fitness routine.
arts_persona: Enthralled by Marathi Natya, classical Hindustani music, and crochet, Dhok Jayashree often listens to recordings of Pandit Bhimsen Joshi, they enjoy Marathi theatre performances, and they mentor senior women in traditional folk songs and craft sessions.
travel_persona: Planning modest trips, Dhok Jayashree enjoys day‑excurisons to Shirdi and Mahabaleshwar, prefers budget stays, and they dream of a pilgrimage tour to Varanasi and Jaipur's heritage sites.
culinary_persona: Passionate about Maharashtrian cuisine, Dhok Jayashree savors puran poli, misal pav, and aamti, relishes the tang of kokum and tamarind, and frequently experiments with seasonal vegetables in her garden, though they rarely cook for guests beyond family.
persona: At 54, Dhok Jayashree blends meticulous organization with a curious love for arts, yet their habit of worrying drives them to schedule every activity down to the minute.
cultural_background: Jayashree grew up in the urban town of Bhandara in Maharashtra, steeped in Marathi culture. Her family follows traditional customs, celebrates festivals such as Ganesh Chaturthi, Gudi Padwa and Diwali with great enthusiasm. The household values community ties, respect for elders, and the simple joys of regional cuisine and folk arts like Lavani and Powada. Being a widow, she continues to maintain close bonds with her extended family and neighbours, embodying the warm hospitality typical of the Marathi middle‑class. She is also curious about art, often visiting local craft fairs and exhibitions.
linguistic_background: Jayashree's mother tongue is Marathi. She can speak, read, and write Marathi fluently, having completed middle school where Marathi was the medium of instruction. She possesses basic reading skills in Hindi, gleaned from school textbooks and everyday signage, but she rarely writes in Hindi. Her English proficiency is limited to functional reading of simple newspapers and correspondence; she can understand spoken English to a modest degree but prefers to communicate in Marathi.
skills_and_expertise: Jayashree excels at managing household affairs, including budgeting, meal planning, and organizing family events. She is a proficient cook of traditional Maharashtrian dishes such as puran poli, misal pav, and aamti. Her hands are skilled in needlework, particularly crochet and embroidery, and she maintains a modest vegetable garden. Additionally, she volunteers at the local community centre, assisting with senior citizen outreach and cultural programs.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['Household budgeting', 'Maharashtrian cooking', 'Crochet', 'Embroidery', 'Vegetable gardening', 'Community volunteer work']
hobbies_and_interests: Jayashree enjoys listening to classical Hindustani music and Marathi Natya, reading Marathi novels, especially works by V.S. Khandekar. She loves gardening, growing seasonal vegetables and flowers, and experimenting with traditional recipes. She also attends local art exhibitions, enjoys crocheting colorful scarves, and practices simple yoga for flexibility.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Listening to classical Hindustani music', 'Reading Marathi literature', 'Gardening', 'Traditional cooking experimentation', 'Attending art exhibitions', 'Crocheting scarves', 'Practicing yoga']
career_goals_and_ambitions: Although retired, Jayashree aspires to stay intellectually active by leading a community art club for senior women, teaching traditional Marathi folk songs and crafts. She aims to publish a small booklet of her family’s favorite recipes, and to continue mentoring younger relatives in household management. Her long‑term ambition is to ensure her cultural heritage is passed on while maintaining her own health through regular yoga and walking.
persona_clinical_summary: A 54-year-old female, widowed, retired homemaker from Bhandara, Maharashtra, with a middle education, who speaks Marathi fluently and has basic Hindi reading skills. She lives in an urban setting and follows traditional Marathi customs, celebrating festivals like Ganesh Chaturthi and Diwali. Clinically, she has a meticulous personality, may experience anxiety, and engages in regular yoga and walking for fitness.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

प्रक्रिया / निष्कर्ष: रुग्ण ही 54 वर्षांची, विडो झालेली महिला गृहिणी आहे, जी भंडारा, महाराष्ट्र येथील आहे. तिच्या डाव्या हातावर एक मोठा, अल्सर झालेला गाठ आहे, ज्याचा क्लिनिकल संशय सॉफ्ट टिश्यू सार्कोमा असा आहे. रुग्णाला इन्सिजनल बायोप्सी आणि वाईड लोकल एक्साईजनसाठी ऑपरेशन थिएटरमध्ये नेण्यात आले. जनरल ॲनेस्थेशिया अंतर्गत, गाठीवर रेखांशाचा छेद (longitudinal incision) देण्यात आला. जखम (lesion) घट्ट, फॅशियाला जोडलेली आणि सुमारे 8x6 सेमी मोजमापाची आढळली. 2 सेमी मार्जिनसह वाईड लोकल एक्साईजन करण्यात आले, सोबतच सेंटिनेल लिम्फ नोड बायोप्सी देखील केली. नमुना हिस्टोपॅथॉलॉजिकल तपासणीसाठी पाठवण्यात आला. जखम ॲबसॉर्बेबल स्युचर्सने थरांमध्ये बंद करण्यात आली. रुग्णाने ही प्रक्रिया चांगल्या प्रकारे सहन केली आणि तिला पोस्ट-ऑपरेटिव्ह वॉर्डमध्ये चांगल्या स्थितीत हलवण्यात आले. तिच्या [[RELATIVE_NAME|Son, Sunil Patil]] ला प्रक्रियेबद्दल आणि बायोप्सी निकालांच्या प्रतीक्षेत असलेल्या पुढील व्यवस्थापनाच्या योजनेबद्दल माहिती देण्यात आली. शस्त्रक्रियेनंतरच्या सूचना जखमेची काळजी आणि फॉलो-अप संदर्भात देण्यात आल्या.
[[PATIENT_ID|PID-BHN-2024-0891]]
[[MRN|MRN-BHN-2024-0891]]
[[BED_NUMBER|15]]
generated_text_en:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Bhandara District Hospital]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-1025]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Savitri Bai Patil]] [[AGE|54]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Deshmukh]]
Procedure / findings: The patient is a 54-year-old widowed female homemaker from Bhandara, Maharashtra, presenting with a large, ulcerated mass on the left forearm, clinically suspected to be a soft tissue sarcoma. The patient was taken to the operating theatre for an incisional biopsy and wide local excision. Under general anesthesia, a longitudinal incision was made over the mass. The lesion was found to be firm, fixed to the underlying fascia, and measured approximately 8x6 cm. A wide local excision with a 2 cm margin was performed, along with a sentinel lymph node biopsy. The specimen was sent for histopathological examination. The wound was closed in layers with absorbable sutures. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was shifted to the post-operative ward in good condition. Her [[RELATIVE_NAME|Son, Sunil Patil]] was informed of the procedure and the plan for further management pending biopsy results. Post-operative instructions were given regarding wound care and follow-up.
[[PATIENT_ID|PID-BHN-2024-0891]]
[[MRN|MRN-BHN-2024-0891]]
[[BED_NUMBER|15]]

professional_persona: Diyal Kaur is a seasoned homemaker who expertly coordinates a large rural household, meticulously planning meals, budgeting groceries, overseeing their children's education, and orchestrating community seva events; their competitive streak drives them to explore a home-based pickling venture, while their cautious nature keeps them vigilant about every detail.
linguistic_persona: Diyal Kaur speaks Punjabi fluently and converses comfortably in Hindi, yet lacks the ability to read or write in either language, and they rely on limited exposure to spoken English from television and relatives to understand basic conversations.
sports_persona: Diyal Kaur follows the IPL team Punjab Kings with enthusiasm, they cheer for them on television, they participate in a local women's walking group for morning exercise, and they occasionally join a village kabaddi session during festivals.
arts_persona: Diyal Kaur delights in folk tunes by Gurdas Maan and Surjit Bindrakhia, enjoys classic ghazals of Jagjit Singh, and often watches popular TV serials featuring actors like Divyanka Tripathi, while they also practice traditional embroidery inspired by phulkari motifs.
travel_persona: Diyal Kaur prefers practical weekend getaways to nearby towns like Amritsar and Chandigarh for family visits, while dreaming of a pilgrimage tour to historic sites across India and an occasional overseas trip to Canada to meet relatives, but they carefully balance travel costs with their household budget.
culinary_persona: Diyal Kaur has a knack for cooking classic dishes like Sarson da Saag, Makki di Roti, Aloo Paratha, and Kadhi Pakora, relishes the aroma of fresh fenugreek leaves, and is experimenting with pickling vegetables for a future home-based venture, though they often rely on their mother’s recipes and prefer family meals over dining out.
persona: Diyal Kaur is an organized, vigilant homemaker with a competitive edge, who subtly worries about finances while channeling their curiosity into home-based entrepreneurial ideas.
cultural_background: Growing up in the rural villages around Faridkot, Diyal Kaur has internalised the deep‑rooted Punjabi Sikh traditions that centre on community service, Gurdwara attendance, and agrarian rhythms, while also adapting to the modest modern conveniences that have entered the countryside.
linguistic_background: She speaks Punjabi fluently as her mother tongue and is comfortable conversing in Hindi, though she never learned to read or write any of these languages. Her limited exposure to English comes from television and occasional interaction with relatives, so she can understand spoken English but cannot read or write it.
skills_and_expertise: Her expertise lies in managing a large rural household efficiently – she plans meals, oversees budgeting for groceries and utilities, coordinates children's schooling, handles basic sewing and mending, assists neighbours during harvest, and organises community seva events at the Gurdwara.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['household management', 'Punjabi cooking', 'budgeting', 'childcare', 'basic sewing', 'agricultural assistance', 'community seva coordination', 'event planning for Gurdwara']
hobbies_and_interests: She enjoys listening to Punjabi folk music and ghazals, watching Punjabi TV serials, gardening in the family courtyard, knitting and embroidery, preparing traditional dishes, and occasionally attending kirtan sessions at the Gurdwara.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['listening to Punjabi folk music', 'watching Punjabi TV serials', 'gardening', 'knitting and embroidery', 'traditional cooking', 'attending Gurdwara kirtan']
career_goals_and_ambitions: As a homemaker, she aspires to support her children's higher education, perhaps start a small home‑based pickling or hand‑loom venture, and enroll in a government adult literacy programme to gain basic reading and writing skills. She also wishes to deepen her involvement in Gurdwara seva and become a trusted point of contact for village women during health awareness drives.
persona_clinical_summary: A 38-year-old female homemaker from rural Faridkot, Punjab, who is currently married and has no formal occupation. She is illiterate, speaks Punjabi as her first language, and has limited English proficiency. Her lifestyle includes managing household finances, community seva, and pursuing home-based entrepreneurial ideas.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

user_prompt:
Write one complete ASHA Worker Note for clinical domain Psychiatry / Behavioral Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Punjabi (code=pa, script=Gurmukhi). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 38
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Rural
- state: Punjab
- district: Faridkot
- first_language: Punjabi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 38-year-old female homemaker from rural Faridkot, Punjab, who is currently married and has no formal occupation. She is illiterate, speaks Punjabi as her first language, and has limited English proficiency. Her lifestyle includes managing household finances, community seva, and pursuing home-based entrepreneurial ideas.

Document format guide for ASHA Worker Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
ASHA note — Village [[VILLAGE|...]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Beneficiary [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|26]] / [[GENDER|Female]]
ASHA: [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|...]] | Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Visit findings: <2-4 short sentences>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- ASHA_WORKER_NAME — ASHA Worker Name e.g. [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
- VILLAGE — Village e.g. [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- BPL_RATION_CARD — BPL / Ration Card Number e.g. [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
- VOTER_ID — Voter ID e.g. [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
- RELIGION — Religion e.g. [[RELIGION|Hindu]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

She is organized and sociable, often hosting family gatherings and managing health-related community outreach.

professional_persona: Ramesh Krishnan, a seasoned car driver in Perambalur, blends precise route navigation and basic mechanical know-how with a friendly bedside manner, using their curiosity to spot shortcut routes and their relaxed approach to keep schedules fluid yet reliable.
linguistic_persona: Ramesh Krishnan grew up speaking Tamil fluently and reading Tamil script, has functional English for basic paperwork and navigation, and understands a handful of Hindi phrases but cannot read or write Hindi.
sports_persona: Ramesh Krishnan loves playing weekend cricket with friends at the local Perambalur ground, cheers for the Tamil Nadu Ranji team and the Chennai Super Kings in IPL, and maintains a modest fitness routine of morning jogs around the village fields.
arts_persona: Ramesh Krishnan immerses themselves in Tamil folk music, enjoys films starring Rajinikanth and Vijay, collects vintage Tamil comic books featuring characters like Varuthapadatha Valibar Sangam, and often photographs vibrant temple murals and local craft workshops.
travel_persona: Ramesh Krishnan prefers spontaneous road trips during off-peak seasons, often explores nearby hill stations like Yercaud and Kodaikanal, dreams of visiting historic Hampi, and balances travel plans with family responsibilities and limited budget.
culinary_persona: Ramesh Krishnan delights in enjoying idli-sambar and masala dosa at midday, savors spicy chicken biryani and mango pickle from roadside stalls, can whip up a simple tomato rasam at home but prefers to eat out, and always looks out for fresh coconut chutney as a must-have side.
persona: Ramesh Krishnan is a curious, sociable driver who balances a love for Tamil folk culture with a laid-back approach to work, finances, and health, often juggling spontaneous plans and a penchant for street food.
cultural_background: Ramesh Krishnan hails from the rural heart of Perambalur in Tamil Nadu, where the agrarian lifestyle, Tamil traditions and Hindu festivals shape everyday life. His family belongs to a Tamil‑speaking Hindu community that balances the region’s farming ethos with modern aspirations. Growing up in a close‑knit village, he was exposed to local folk arts, temple celebrations and the values of cooperation and hospitality that are common in Tamil culture. His natural curiosity drives him to appreciate the artistic expressions he encounters, from mural paintings to folk songs.
linguistic_background: Ramesh grew up speaking Tamil at home and in his village, and he is fully literate in Tamil. He completed middle school, which gave him a functional grasp of basic English for reading signs and filling simple paperwork, but he does not use English regularly. He has little exposure to Hindi, so he can understand a few spoken phrases but cannot read or write Hindi.
skills_and_expertise: Ramesh is a professional car driver with strong expertise in safe driving, route navigation, and routine vehicle maintenance. He can perform basic mechanical checks, handle cash and receipts, provide courteous customer service, and adapt quickly to traffic changes using his mobile navigation apps. His sociable nature helps him communicate effectively with passengers, and his flexibility allows him to manage irregular schedules and spontaneous assignments.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Car driving", "Vehicle maintenance", "Customer service", "Route navigation", "Basic mechanical troubleshooting", "Cash handling", "Friendly communication", "Adaptability to schedule changes"]
hobbies_and_interests: Ramesh enjoys listening to Tamil folk music, watching Tamil movies, reading comic books, taking photos of rural scenery with his mobile phone, playing cricket with friends on weekends, socialising during temple festivals, exploring street food stalls on his routes, and learning about local art and crafts.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Listening to Tamil folk music", "Watching Tamil movies", "Reading comic books", "Mobile photography of rural landscapes", "Playing cricket", "Socialising at temple festivals", "Exploring street food", "Learning about local art and crafts"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Ramesh aims to upgrade his driving career by obtaining a commercial driving licence for larger vehicles, become a senior driver for a reputable transport company, and eventually start his own mini‑bus service connecting Perambalur with nearby towns. He also wishes to deepen his mechanical knowledge to run a small roadside garage, ensuring a steady income for his family and better educational opportunities for his children.
persona_clinical_summary: A 33-year-old male driver from Perambalur, Tamil Nadu, with a middle school education, currently married, and Tamil-speaking. He works in the rural zone and has a functional grasp of basic English. His lifestyle includes a love for street food and spontaneous travel, with occasional cricket and a preference for local Tamil cultural activities.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.

generated_text:
OPD சீட்டு | பெரம்பலூர் மாவட்ட மருத்துவமனை | அடையாள எண் [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-PER-001]]
நோயாளி: [[PATIENT_NAME|Karthik]] | பிறந்த தேதி [[DOB|1991-08-22]] | வயது: [[AGE|33]] | பாலினம்: [[GENDER|Male]]
தொழில்: [[OCCUPATION|Driver, Car]] | மருத்துவ பதிவு எண்: [[MRN|OPD-2024-0815-001]] | மருத்துவர்: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. S. Ramesh]]
மாவட்டம்: [[DISTRICT|Perambalur]] | முதன்மைப் புகார்: மூன்று வாரங்களாகத் தொடரும் இருமல் மற்றும் குறைந்த அளவிலான காய்ச்சல்.

தற்போதைய நோயின் வரலாறு:
பெரம்பலூரைச் சேர்ந்த 33 வயது ஆண் கார் ஓட்டுநர், மூன்று வாரங்களாக இருமல் மற்றும் சளியுடன் வருகிறார். ஆரம்பத்தில் வறட்டு இருமல் இருந்த நிலையில், தற்போது மிகக் குறைந்த அளவில் வெள்ளை நிறச் சளி வெளியேறுகிறது. மாலை நேரங்களில் குறைந்த அளவிலான காய்ச்சல், அவ்வப்போது இரவு நேர வியர்வை மற்றும் கடந்த ஒரு மாதத்தில் 5 கிலோ உடல் எடை குறைவு ஆகியவற்றை அவர் தெரிவிக்கிறார். இரத்தத்துடன் சளி வெளியேறுதல் (hemoptysis), மார்பு வலி அல்லது மூச்சுத் திணறல் போன்ற பாதிப்புகள் ஏதுமில்லை. கடந்த 10 ஆண்டுகளாகத் தினமும் 10 சிகரெட் பிடிக்கும் பழக்கம் கொண்டவர். கார் ஓட்டுநராகப் பணியாற்றுவதோடு, கிராமப்புறத்தில் வசிக்கிறார். அடிப்படை ஆங்கிலத்தைப் புரிந்துகொள்ளும் திறன் கொண்டவர்.

உடல் பரிசோதனை:
பொதுவான நிலை: நனநிலை மற்றும் விழிப்புணர்வுடன், நடுத்தர உடல்வாகு கொண்டவர், நாள்பட்ட நோயால் பாதிக்கப்பட்டவர் போலத் தெரிகிறார்.
முக்கிய அறிகுறிகள்: இரத்த அழுத்தம் 120/80 mmHg, இதயத் துடிப்பு நிமிடத்திற்கு 98, சுவாச வீதம் நிமிடத்திற்கு 20, உடல் வெப்பநிலை 37.8°C, அறை காற்றில் SpO2 96%.
மார்பு: இருபுறமும் காற்றுச் செலுத்தம் சீராக உள்ளது, வெசிகுலர் சுவாச ஒலிகள் உள்ளன, மூச்சுச் சீறல் (wheeze) அல்லது கிரெபிடேஷன் (crepitations) இல்லை. விரல் நுனி வீக்கம் (clubbing) அல்லது நிணநீர் முடிச்சுகள் வீக்கம் (lymphadenopathy) ஏதுமில்லை.

ஆய்வகப் பரிசோதனைகள்:
AFB-க்கான சளிப் பரிசோதனை: நேர்மறை (2+).
மார்பு எக்ஸ்-ரே: நுரையீரல் காசநோய் இருப்பதைக் குறிக்கும் வகையில் இருபுறமும் ஹிலார் நிணநீர் முடிச்சுகள் வீக்கம் மற்றும் திசு ஊடுருவல்கள் (parenchymal infiltrates) காணப்படுகின்றன.
இரத்த அணுக்கள் பரிசோதனை (CBC): Hb 11.2 g/dL, TLC 8500/cu.mm, தட்டணுக்கள் (Platelets) 2.5 லட்சம்/cu.mm.

நோய் கண்டறிதல்:
நுரையீரல் காசநோய், புதிய பாதிப்பு.

சிகிச்சைத் திட்டம்:
திருத்தப்பட்ட தேசிய காசநோய் கட்டுப்பாட்டுத் திட்டத்தின் (RNTCP) வழிகாட்டுதல்களின்படி, வகை I DOTS சிகிச்சை முறை தொடங்கப்பட்டது.
- ஐசோனியாசிட் (Isoniazid) 300 மி.கி. தினமும்
- ரிஃபாம்பின் (Rifampicin) 450 மி.கி. தினமும்
- பைராசினமைடு (Pyrazinamide) 1500 மி.கி. தினமும்
- எதம்புட்டால் (Ethambutol) 750 மி.கி. தினமும்

சிகிச்சையை முறையாகப் பின்பற்றுதல், பக்கவிளைவுகள் மற்றும் முழுமையான சிகிச்சையை முடிப்பதன் முக்கியத்துவம் குறித்து நோயாளிக்கு ஆலோசனைகள் வழங்கப்பட்டன. நேரடியாகக் கண்காணிக்கப்படும் சிகிச்சையைப் (DOTS) பெறுவதற்காக, வாரத்திற்கு மூன்று முறை DOTS மையத்திற்கு வர அறிவுறுத்தப்பட்டார்.

தொடர் கண்காணிப்பு:
இரண்டு வாரங்களில் மீண்டும் பரிசோதிக்கப்பட வேண்டும்.

தொடர்புக்கு: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
உறவினர் தொடர்பு: [[RELATIVE_NAME|Lakshmi]]
generated_text_en:
OPD Slip | District Hospital Perambalur | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-PER-001]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|Karthik]] | DOB [[DOB|1991-08-22]] | Age: [[AGE|33]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Male]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Driver, Car]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-0815-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. S. Ramesh]]
District: [[DISTRICT|Perambalur]] | Chief complaint: Persistent cough for three weeks with low-grade fever.

History of Present Illness:
Patient is a 33-year-old male driver from Perambalur, Tamil Nadu, who presents with a three-week history of a productive cough, initially dry and now with scant white sputum. He reports low-grade evening fevers, occasional night sweats, and a 5 kg weight loss over the past month. He denies hemoptysis, chest pain, or shortness of breath. He has a history of smoking 10 cigarettes per day for the last 10 years. He works as a car driver and lives in a rural area. He has a functional grasp of basic English.

Physical Examination:
General: Conscious and oriented, moderately built, appears chronically ill.
Vitals: BP 120/80 mmHg, Pulse 98/min, Respiratory Rate 20/min, Temperature 37.8°C, SpO2 96% on room air.
Chest: Bilateral air entry, vesicular breath sounds, no wheeze or crepitations. No clubbing or lymphadenopathy.

Investigations:
Sputum for AFB: Positive (2+).
Chest X-ray: Bilateral hilar lymphadenopathy with parenchymal infiltrates suggestive of pulmonary tuberculosis.
CBC: Hb 11.2 g/dL, TLC 8500/cu.mm, Platelets 2.5 lakhs/cu.mm.

Diagnosis:
Pulmonary Tuberculosis, new case.

Treatment Plan:
Started on Category I DOTS regimen as per Revised National Tuberculosis Control Programme (RNTCP) guidelines.
- Isoniazid 300 mg daily
- Rifampicin 450 mg daily
- Pyrazinamide 1500 mg daily
- Ethambutol 750 mg daily

Patient counseled on treatment adherence, side effects, and the importance of completing the full course. He was advised to visit the DOTS center thrice a week for directly observed therapy.

Follow-up:
Review in 2 weeks.

Contact for follow-up: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
Relative Contact: [[RELATIVE_NAME|Lakshmi]]

professional_persona: Harish Nan is a veteran cooper who blends precise barrel‑making and crane‑operation expertise with a curious, solitary mindset, constantly seeking to modernise the workshop with a mechanised press while mentoring their son.
linguistic_persona: Harish Nan speaks Tamil fluently and reads and writes it with ease, while their English is functional for listening and simple conversation but limited in reading and writing, and they do not speak Hindi although they recognize occasional Hindi words in chants.
sports_persona: Harish Nan enjoys watching cricket, cheering for the Chennai Super Kings in the IPL, follows Tamil Nadu's Ranji team, occasionally plays informal cricket with village mates on weekends, and maintains basic fitness through daily walks in the paddy fields and occasional morning yoga, preferring solitary exercise over gym crowds.
arts_persona: Harish Nan spends evenings sketching traditional motifs and carving wooden figurines, draws inspiration from legendary Carnatic vocalist M.S. Subbulakshmi and folk artist K. Kalyanasundaram, experiments with experimental painting on reclaimed wood, and occasionally visits the Tirumalai art center to view contemporary works, reflecting a blend of reverence for heritage and curiosity for modern art.
travel_persona: Harish Nan prefers modest, well‑planned trips, often traveling by train to nearby temples in Kanchipuram and Tirupati, enjoys weekend getaways to the hills of Yelagiri for quiet walks, and dreams of a pilgrimage to Varanasi to experience its sacred ghats, but limits travel due to family responsibilities and budget constraints.
culinary_persona: Harish Nan enjoys traditional South Indian dishes such as sambar‑laden rice, pongal, rasam, and hearty idli‑dosa with coconut chutney, prefers authentic flavors like mustard seeds, curry leaves, and tamarind, rarely cooks himself but relishes meals prepared by his wife, and occasionally grills fresh fish with lemon and pepper during festive evenings.
persona: Harish Nan is a solitary, curious craftsman who balances meticulous barrel‑making with artistic wood carving, worries about financial stability, and navigates life with a blend of traditional discipline and a quiet drive for innovation.
cultural_background: Harish Nan grew up in a small, agrarian Tamil village on the outskirts of Vellore, Tamil Nadu. The village life revolves around the cycles of the paddy fields, Pongal celebrations, and the rhythmic hum of traditional folk songs. Coming from a family of cooper‑makers, he inherited the craft of barrel‑making and a deep respect for hands‑on workmanship. The close‑knit community, rooted in Tamil Hindu customs, emphasizes familial duties, modest living, and participation in temple festivals. While his environment is modest and rooted in tradition, Harish’s curiosity drives him to seek new ideas and artistic expressions beyond the village boundaries.
linguistic_background: Harish’s mother tongue is Tamil; he is fully literate in Tamil, having completed primary schooling in his native language. His formal English education stopped at the primary level, so his English proficiency is functional for listening to work instructions and simple conversation, but reading and writing in English remain limited. He does not speak Hindi, though he can recognize common Hindi words encountered in religious chants and occasional signage at the plant.
skills_and_expertise: Harish has extensive hands‑on experience as a cooper, crafting barrels and wooden vessels with precision. He is adept at operating cranes, hoists, and related plant machinery, handling hydraulic controls and ensuring safety compliance. His mechanical aptitude includes routine maintenance, troubleshooting of engines and hydraulic systems, and metalworking for repair work. He also possesses carpentry skills for constructing and repairing wooden fixtures, and basic workshop management abilities such as inventory tracking and tool organization.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Cooper (barrel making)", "Crane and hoist operation", "Plant equipment maintenance", "Mechanical troubleshooting", "Metalworking", "Carpentry", "Basic workshop management"]
hobbies_and_interests: Harish enjoys solitary creative pursuits that feed his curiosity about art. He often sketches traditional Tamil motifs and engages in wood‑carving, crafting small figurines or decorative panels for temples. He listens to Carnatic classical music while working, finding it calming. He reads books on Indian craft traditions and watches documentary videos on folk art during his free evenings. Gardening in his courtyard, taking quiet walks around the paddy fields, and solving chess puzzles are other ways he unwinds. Occasionally, he experiments with experimental painting on reclaimed wood, blending his love for tradition with new ideas.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Sketching traditional Tamil motifs", "Wood carving", "Listening to Carnatic music", "Reading books on Indian crafts", "Watching documentary films on folk art", "Gardening", "Solitary walks in fields", "Solving chess puzzles", "Experimental painting on reclaimed wood"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: At 48, Harish aims to secure a stable and dignified livelihood for his family while gradually transitioning from physically demanding tasks to a supervisory role. He wishes to formalize his cooper workshop, acquire a small mechanised press for producing wooden containers, and obtain certification in crane operation safety. He also aspires to mentor his son in the craft, ensuring the family trade continues. Additionally, he hopes to allocate time for his personal artistic pursuits, perhaps by creating bespoke wooden art pieces for local festivals, thereby blending his curiosity for art with his livelihood.
persona_clinical_summary: Mr. Harish Nan, a 48-year-old male cooper from rural Vellore, Tamil Nadu, is married with primary education and speaks Tamil. He works as a cooper, balancing traditional barrel-making with modern workshop innovations. His lifestyle includes daily walks and occasional informal cricket with village mates.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.
- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

user_prompt:
Write one complete Surgical / Operative Note for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Tamil (code=ta, script=Tamil). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 48
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Cooper
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Rural
- state: Tamil Nadu
- district: Vellore
- first_language: Tamil
- clinical_persona_summary: Mr. Harish Nan, a 48-year-old male cooper from rural Vellore, Tamil Nadu, is married with primary education and speaks Tamil. He works as a cooper, balancing traditional barrel-making with modern workshop innovations. His lifestyle includes daily walks and occasional informal cricket with village mates.

Document format guide for Surgical / Operative Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Operative note — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|OT]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|50]] [[GENDER|Female]] Surgeon [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Procedure / findings: MUST describe an actual procedure/operation matching the
assigned clinical domain (not a psychiatry admission note). Keep relative names
gender-plausible.

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
